pairs.question: When and where was Daniel Vranes born?
pairs.answer: Daniel LaDrew Vranes was born on October 29, 1958, in the United States.

pairs.question: Which high school did Daniel Vranes attend, and what achievement did he accomplish there?
pairs.answer: Vranes attended Skyline High School in Salt Lake City, where he led his team to the state basketball championship in his senior year (1977).

pairs.question: What notable basketball team was Daniel Vranes a part of during his college years?
pairs.answer: Vranes played college basketball for the University of Utah.

pairs.question: In which NBA draft was Daniel Vranes selected, and by which team?
pairs.answer: Vranes was selected in the 1st round (5th overall) of the 1981 NBA draft by the Seattle SuperSonics.

pairs.question: How many NBA seasons did Daniel Vranes play, and which teams did he play for?
pairs.answer: Vranes played in seven NBA seasons, from 1981 to 1988, for the Seattle SuperSonics and the Philadelphia 76ers.

pairs.question: What is Daniel Vranes' career-high in rebounds in a single NBA game, and when did he achieve it?
pairs.answer: Vranes' career high in a single game is 18 rebounds (nine offensive and nine defensive), which he achieved on April 22, 1983, during a playoff loss to the Portland Trail Blazers.

pairs.question: During the 1983–84 NBA season, what were Daniel Vranes' average points per game and total games played?
pairs.answer: During the 1983–84 season, Vranes averaged 8.4 points per game and played in 80 games.

pairs.question: What defensive achievement did Daniel Vranes accomplish after the 1984–85 NBA season?
pairs.answer: After the 1984–85 season, Vranes was named to the NBA All-Defensive Second Team for his defensive performance.

pairs.question: Which European basketball teams did Daniel Vranes play for during his career?
pairs.answer: Vranes played in Europe for AEK BC, Teorematour Arese, and Breeze Milano.
